episodes.title: Andrew Lownie on The Monarchy & The Fall on The House of York.
episodes.description:
In this episode of People & Politics, I’m joined by  historian and biographer Andrew Lownie for a  conversation about his latest book, Entitled — The Rise and Fall of The House of York. This book is  an investigation into the former Prince Andrew and what his story reveals about the monarchy, privilege, and accountability in modern Britain.

In our conversation, we explore:

•  The former Prince Andrew’s arrogance and entitlement in his life, relationships, and public role
•  How the monarchy avoids scrutiny, and public accountability 
•  The systems that protect powerful individuals from consequences
•  What this story tells us about trust, transparency, and the future of the institution

This is a grounded, accessible discussion that cuts through speculation and focuses on evidence, context, and the structures that shape public life.

🎙️ People & Politics is a podcast committed to widening who gets to take part in political conversation — especially those who rarely see themselves represented. Hosted by David Reilly.
If you find the episode thought‑provoking, please like, comment, and subscribe. It genuinely helps the show reach more people.
